```json [{"bbox": [68, 66, 127, 91], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [813, 66, 930, 96], "category": "header", "text": "STUSB4500\nPin description"}, {"bbox": [162, 120, 926, 162], "category": "text", "text": "- At detachment the POWER_OK pins remain enabled (if already asserted), whereas VBUS_EN_SNK is disabled (Hi-Z) to deactivate the $V_{BUS}$ power path from the USB Type-C receptacle. The POWER_OK pins state is reinitialized (Hi-Z) after new attachment or after a reset"}, {"bbox": [67, 177, 212, 191], "category": "title", "text": "2.2.9 GPIO"}, {"bbox": [161, 194, 822, 221], "category": "text", "text": "This pin is an active low open drain output that can be configured by NVM as per table below (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)."}, {"bbox": [432, 240, 661, 254], "category": "table_caption", "text": "Table 5. GPIO pin configuration"}, {"bbox": [164, 260, 927, 507], "category": "table", "text": "
NVM parameter GPIO_CFG[1:0]Pin namePin functionValueDescription
00bSW_CTRL_GPIOSoftware controlled GPIO. The output state is defined by the value of I²C register bit #0 at address 2DhHi-ZWhen bit #0 value is 0b (at start-up)
0When bit #0 value is 1b
01b (default)ERROR_RECOVERYHardware fault detection (see Section 3.7 Hardware fault management)Hi-ZNo hardware fault detected
0Hardware fault detected
10bDEBUGDebug accessory detection (see Section 3.8 Debug accessory mode detection)Hi-ZNo debug accessory detected
0Debug accessory detected
11bSINK_POWERIndicates USB Type-C current capability advertised by the sourceHi-ZSource supplies default or 1.5 A USB Type-C current at 5 V
0Source supplies 3.0 A USB Type-C current at 5 V
"}, {"bbox": [67, 525, 294, 540], "category": "title", "text": "2.2.10 VBUS_EN_SNK"}, {"bbox": [161, 543, 902, 585], "category": "text", "text": "This pin allows the incoming $V_{BUS}$ power from the USB Type-C receptacle to be enabled when a source is connected according to different operating conditions stated in the table below. The default behavior of the pin can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)."}, {"bbox": [395, 603, 698, 617], "category": "table_caption", "text": "Table 6. VBUS_EN_SNK pin configuration"}, {"bbox": [164, 622, 927, 794], "category": "table", "text": "
NVM parameter POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5VPin functionValueDescriptionComment
0b (default)Enables \\(V_{BUS}\\) power path when source attached whatever \\(V_{BUS}\\) voltage (5 V or any PDO voltage)Hi-ZNo source attachedValid for all POWER_OK pin configurations 1, 2 and 3
0Source attached
1bEnables \\(V_{BUS}\\) power path only when source attached and \\(V_{BUS}\\) voltage negotiated to PDO2 or PDO3 voltageHi-ZNo source attached or no PD explicit contract with PDO2 or PDO3
0Source attached and PD explicit contract with PDO2 or PDO3
"}, {"bbox": [161, 811, 915, 839], "category": "text", "text": "When POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5V bit is set to logic level high, the VBUS_EN_SNK pin is asserted only when a PDO2 or PDO3 explicit contract is established with the source (see Section 3.3 Auto-run mode)."}, {"bbox": [161, 841, 918, 868], "category": "text", "text": "This feature is suited for sink devices requiring high power charging profile above 5 V to be fully operational (see Section 6.1.2 Powering a system under high charging profile only)."}, {"bbox": [161, 869, 894, 897], "category": "text", "text": "VBUS_EN_SNK pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to enable the $V_{BUS}$ power path."}, {"bbox": [68, 973, 164, 984], "category": "footer", "text": "DS12499 - Rev 8"}, {"bbox": [872, 974, 930, 986], "category": "footer", "text": "page 8/42"}] ```
